Class DeviationService

java.lang.Object
ntnu.no.fs_v26.service.DeviationService
@Service public class DeviationService extends Object
Service class for deviation report management.

Handles creation, retrieval, and status updates of deviation reports. All operations are scoped to the authenticated user's organization. Status updates are restricted to users with the MANAGER or ADMIN role.

    • DeviationService

      public DeviationService()

  • Method Details

    • getDeviations

      public org.springframework.data.domain.Page<Deviation> getDeviations(User user, int page, int size)
      Returns a paginated list of deviations for the user's organization, sorted by creation date descending.
      Parameters:
      user - the authenticated user
      page - the page number (zero-indexed)
      size - the number of items per page
      Returns:
      a page of deviations for the organization

    • getAllDeviations

      public List<Deviation> getAllDeviations(User user)
      Returns all deviations for the user's organization. Used by report generation.
      Parameters:
      user - the authenticated user
      Returns:
      a list of all deviations for the organization

    • reportDeviation

      public Deviation reportDeviation(DeviationRequest request, User user)
      Creates and saves a new deviation report for the user's organization.

      Uses a DeviationRequest DTO to prevent mass-assignment — fields such as status, organization, reportedBy, and timestamps are set server-side and cannot be influenced by the caller.

      Parameters:
      request - the validated request body
      user - the authenticated user reporting the deviation
      Returns:
      the saved deviation with status DeviationStatus.OPEN

    • updateStatus

      public Deviation updateStatus(Long id, DeviationStatus newStatus, User user)
      Updates the status of an existing deviation.

      Verifies that the deviation belongs to the user's organization before updating. If the new status is DeviationStatus.RESOLVED, the resolved timestamp is set automatically.

      Parameters:
      id - the ID of the deviation to update
      newStatus - the new status to set
      user - the authenticated manager or admin
      Returns:
      the updated deviation
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the deviation is not found
      org.springframework.security.access.AccessDeniedException - if the deviation belongs to a different organization
